articles.title: Trump says US secretly helped ships move oil through Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US president says his military has been running a "secret mission" to help tankers and commercial ships defy Iran's blockade.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits in a leather chair.

articles.title: Sea drone saves US helicopter crew downed in waters near Strait of Hormuz

children.content: A look at the US Corsair sea drone used in a first of its kind mission to rescue two helicopter crew shot down near the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: A sea drone that looks like a high-tech speed boat skimming across the water.

children.content: The US military says it has launched a series of strikes on "multiple targets" in Iran. Follow live.

items.title: US launches another round of strikes against Iran after Trump threatens to attack 'very hard'

updates.title: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz after US attacks
US military confirms another round of strikes against Iran
Iranian media reports explosions across the country

items.title: Albanese's frustration with Trump is becoming harder to hide

children.content: The Albanese government is paying an economic and political toll for an ongoing and deeply unpopular war.

image.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: One Nation fundraising drive receives $1.6m in donations

children.content: One Nation says it has received $1.6 million in donations after launching a drive in response to a Labor fundraising campaign. Follow live.

image.altText: pauline hanson speaking in front of a justice for ben banner

items.title: What happens when a gas company abandons an entire city?

children.content: For many, getting off gas and electrifying their homes is a personal choice. But what happens when an entire town is abandoned by a gas company?

image.altText: Bearded man wearing black polo shirt standing at a stainless steel counter in a professional kitchen

items.title: Taylor rules out non-compete agreement with One Nation as party raises $1.6m

children.content: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the Coalition has no plan to share the seats the party targets at the next federal election with One Nation as Pauline Hanson's party says it has raised $1.6 million in a day.

image.altText: An older man wearing a suit gives an impassioned speech.

items.title: Stabbing victim's family calls for calm amid violent protests in Northern Ireland

children.content: The family of a Northern Irish man who lost an eye in a knife attack has appealed for calm ​after anti-immigrant violence in Belfast.

image.altText: Protesters blocking water cannons with bin.

Armoured police stand near an armoured police vehicle spraying water.

items.title: Why the Belfast stabbing could ignite a summer of unrest

children.content: As UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights for his political life, the country is bracing for what could be a yet another summer of unrest.

image.altText: A car on fire on a street literred with an debris and surrounded by buildings.

items.title: Up to 300 jobs cut at Southern Cross Media, ASX set to fall after Wall Street closed down

children.content: Up to 300 jobs are to be cut at Southern Cross Media, which owns the Seven network. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Massive whale graveyard found in deep ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

image.altText: Underwater vision of a whale fossil on the seafloor.

gallery.altText: Two beak whale skulls like very long sharp teeth next to two small skull fragments of the sharper end against black backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Two beak whale skulls like very long sharp teeth next to two small skull fragments of the sharper end against black backdrop.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: Government flags risk of relying on Elon Musk's SpaceX and Starlink

children.content: As tens of thousands of Australians line up to buy shares in Elon Musk's SpaceX blockbuster public offering, Australian government officials are privately flagging the risks posed by the country's growing reliance on its satellite internet service, Starlink.

image.altText: Elon Musk

items.title: 'People are just killing themselves': Solariums operating underground

children.content: Illegal solariums are operating across the country, from single machines secretly set up in suburban neighbourhood garages to those hidden in the back of legitimate businesses.

image.altText: The sillhouette of a woman's side profile with blue lights from a solarium behind her

items.title: Grandfather's fall inspires 20yo student's smart clock invention

children.content: After it took nearly two days for his grandfather to be found following a serious fall, Kai Gould has created a device to connect older people living independently with their loved ones.

gallery.altText: Older man and younger man holding clock
Clock, phone
Man with hands working on electrical board

items.title: Notorious Sydney gang rapist Mohammed Skaf facing drug charges

children.content: NSW Police arrested the 42-year-old in Greenacre in Sydney's south-west overnight and he has been charged with 24 offences.

image.altText: a man in a hugo boss shirt surrounded by police

items.title: 'Very unfair': Collapsed petrol empire lands deal to pay fraction of $13m debt

children.content: The directors of Zoya Investments have agreed to a $2 million settlement to end the liquidator's case against them.

image.altText: Rizwan Rana in a Ralph Lauren polo shirt standing in front of his Lexus.

items.title: In Moldova, I meet a woman with a dangerous plan to save her life

children.content: On a park bench in Moldova, I meet a Ukrainian refugee who is planning to return home through a war zone for cancer treatment. We talk for hours.

gallery.altText: A close up of Tatiana, who has short platimun blonde hair.
A car windscreen with holes in it, two pieces of paper have Ukrainian words printed on them.
The front of a destroyed building.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of Tatiana, who has short platimun blonde hair.
A car windscreen with holes in it, two pieces of paper have Ukrainian words printed on them.
The front of a destroyed building.

items.title: Spielberg's Disclosure Day is a close encounter of the hopeful kind

children.content: ET director Steven Spielberg dives back into the extra terrestrial with his latest film while asking 'How do you astonish an audience when anything is possible, and nothing is real?

image.altText: A woman holds a glowing rod in front of a group of people

items.title: How well do you know Aussie books? Take our quiz to find out

children.content: Occupy yourself with some armchair travel courtesy of a selection of our nation's most esteemed literary works.

image.altText: A young woman wearing shorts and a T-shirt lies in a meadow with yellow flowers, reading a book.

items.title: WA coastal towns lose more beachfront to erosion

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

gallery.altText: Sand bags sit in front a cliff of sand. A building sits a few metres behind.
The ocean in front of a severley eroded dune.
Linda Balcombe standing in front of an eroded sand dune, frowning

thumbnail.altText: Sand bags sit in front a cliff of sand. A building sits a few metres behind.
The ocean in front of a severley eroded dune.
Linda Balcombe standing in front of an eroded sand dune, frowning

items.title: The Aussie leading his class ahead of the legendary 24 Hours of Le Mans

children.content: The 24 Hours of Le Mans is a jewel in the motorsport crown, a bucket-list item for drivers and fans, and the 94th edition of the famous race will be held this weekend.

image.altText: GT3 cars at the Le Mans 24hr preview, driving in the evening down the long straight

items.title: Gout sixth in Diamond League debut as Olympic champ claims victory

children.content: Gout Gout finishes well back in the field in the men's 200 metres at the Oslo Diamond League meet, with Olympic champion Letsile Tebogo first across the line.

image.altText: Gout Gout looks on at the Oslo diamond League meet.

items.title: Pope Leo blesses Barcelona's Sagrada Familia as an 'architectural masterpiece'

children.content: The pontiff has celebrated Spain's Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of "stones, colours and light," as he marked the centenary of the death of its architect Antoni Gaudí.

gallery.altText: The interior hall of the Sagrada Familia illuminated in sunlight between its pylons, over a crowd of people.
The Sagrada Familia at night, illuminted in light with a white cross at its top, surrounded by people in white robes.
Pope Leo and Catholic Church clergy members in white and gold robes standing under large organs and a sculpture of Jesus.

thumbnail.altText: The interior hall of the Sagrada Familia illuminated in sunlight between its pylons, over a crowd of people.
The Sagrada Familia at night, illuminted in light with a white cross at its top, surrounded by people in white robes.
Pope Leo and Catholic Church clergy members in white and gold robes standing under large organs and a sculpture of Jesus.

items.title: Predictive text in 'demonstrable decline' with AI-based language models

children.content: If internet chatter is to be believed, the intuitiveness and efficiency of predictive text and autocorrect have fallen off a cliff in recent years. But while researchers agree there has been a demonstrable decline in quality, the increasing sophistication of what phones can do is also making errors more prominent.

image.altText: Young lady with looks frustrated with phone

items.title: Brother and sister from remote SA town receive King's Birthday Honours

children.content: A brother and sister who grew up near the rural town of Lock on the Eyre Peninsula both received honours over the King's Birthday long weekend.

image.altText: A woman stands next to a sign in a rural area. A paddock is behind her.

items.title: New species found in Australia's most 'pristine' marine parks

children.content: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.

gallery.altText: A bright orange furry crab.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.
A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.

thumbnail.altText: A bright orange furry crab.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.
A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.

items.title: Bakery trains staff to assess counterfeit notes amid rise in use in SA

children.content: An Adelaide business owner who has been targeted by counterfeiters about a dozen times is warning others to be vigilant, as SA Police warn the number of incidents has increased in the state in the past year.

image.altText: A close-up of a hand holding a $50 note and shining an blue UV light on the clear strip.

items.title: Commonwealth Bank pursued in High Court by shareholders

children.content: The applicants want compensation for the "inflated value of their shares attributable to the bank's wrongful failure to inform the market".

image.altText: The entrance to Commonwealth Bank.

items.title: Dark Mofo returns with art, food and sell-out events

children.content: Tasmania's two-week Dark Mofo festival kicks off today in Hobart. Popular events such as Winter Feast, Night Mass, the Nude Solstice Swim and the Ogoh Ogoh procession return, while one of the new Spirit of Tasmania ferries will host a free art exhibition.

image.altText: Illuminated signage with sparks being shot into the air at a festival installation.

items.title: Victorian town fights high suicide rates with mental health first aid

children.content: After losing a family member to suicide, Peter Taylor vowed to bring mental health first aid to his community.

image.altText: a man and a woman sit at a table looking at a document in front of them

items.title: Mexico City welcomes World Cup but reality bites for many

children.content: As Mexicans prepare to host or co-host the FIFA World Cup for a record third time, many locals are frustrated by being locked out of the tournament, while others are more optimistic.

articles.title: A peculiar peace prize and threatened boycotts: The rocky road to the World Cup

children.content: Struggled to keep up with the rollercoaster of controversies that plagued the build-up to this World Cup? We've got you covered.

image.altText: A smiling Donald Trump holds up his FIFA Peace Price medal on stage

articles.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

image.altText: A smiling man lifts a golden trophy among celebrating teammates with fireworks exploding in the background.

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

image.altText: Generic 2026 FIFA World Cup football

items.title: NT attorney-general backs community safety changes despite unrest

children.content: The government says bail law changes made a year ago are working despite a string of tragedies and unrest in bush communities.

image.altText: Close-up of a white woman, blonde hair, wearing a light blue blazer, white blouse, looking intently at camera, soft smile.

items.title: Senator Wendy Askew won't seek re-election. Liberals, One Nation eye seat

children.content: A Tasmanian senator will not recontest her seat in 2028, meaning it is up for the taking by Liberal colleagues and possibly a resurgent One Nation.

image.altText: Middle aged woman with glasses stands in front of Australian flag.

items.title: When 'Island Nemo' went missing, locals suspected foul play

children.content: The little clownfish known as "Island Nemo" was always there for locals and tourists snorkelling in a tropical bay. But then it disappeared and locals suspect foul play.

gallery.altText: A close up of a clownfish

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a clownfish

items.title: Bill Gates 'deeply sorry' for Epstein ties in testimony to US politicians

children.content: The Microsoft co-founder appeared before a closed-door US House Oversight Committee panel that has been investigating the late sex offender's connections with the rich and powerful.

image.altText: Bill Gates walks down a hallway in the US Congress surrounded by other people.

items.title: O'Callaghan chasing Titmus and 'the impossible' as she misses world record

children.content: Mollie O'Callaghan shoots out of the gates in the 200m freestyle but cannot maintain the world record pace as she books her ticket to the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: 'You're isolated out here': Queensland's rapidly expanding suburbs cut off

children.content: Hundreds of thousands of new homes are being built on the outskirts of greater Brisbane. But residents are concerned if one thing doesn't improve, their quality of life will go backwards.

gallery.altText: a woman waits at a bus stop

thumbnail.altText: a woman waits at a bus stop
